materialized_evidence.span: Gradient descent requires the gradient of the objective function. With step size \(\alpha_k>0\), the method moves opposite to the gradient. The gradient descent update is

\[
x_{k+1}=x_k-\alpha_k\nabla f(x_k).
\]

items.rationale: The gradient is used in the gradient descent update formula, which includes the step size. Thus, the gradient is applied in the context of step size.

materialized_evidence.span: For observations \((x_i,y_i)\), the mean squared error formula is

\[
\operatorname{MSE}(\beta)=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}(y_i-x_i^\top\beta)^2.
\]
This formula formalizes the least squares objective up to a positive constant scaling. Gradient descent can be applied to ordinary least squares by using the gradient of this objective function.
